summary: Fitness/health app with PWA support. Step counter feature is being replaced with manual step entry where the user inputs steps and AI calculates calories burned.
known_issues: Step counter (automatic) was broken/non-functional; replaced with manual entry

existing_features.title: Replace the automatic step counter widget in the Fitness section with a manual step entry input field. The user should be able to type in a number of steps directly. Remove or hide the DeviceMotion-based StepTrackerWidget and useStepTracker hook usage from the UI.

When the user enters a step count manually, display an AI-calculated estimate of calories burned based on those steps. Use a standard heuristic (e.g., approximately 0.04–0.05 kcal per step, adjusted by the user's body weight from their fitness profile if available) to compute the estimate. Show the result inline near the step input, updating in real time as the user types.

Persist the manually entered step count to the backend daily metrics (the existing logDailyMetrics mutation) when the user saves or submits the daily metrics form, replacing the auto-filled step value that previously came from the step tracker hook.

new_feature_requests.summary: Replace broken step counter with manual step entry field; AI calculates and displays calories burned based on entered steps
